extracted_json.title: 1.4 Technology Strategy (TS)

items.text: Definitions (in the form of questions) — Example (CISCO)
(i) What are the core technologies that give the company a technological advantage over its competitors
Example: advanced, state-of-the-art Computer Network (CN) Technologies (routers, switches,...) for a wide variety of customers
(ii) What is the company's approach to product & technology development
Example: World Leader in CN Technologies, based on advanced R&D, new product development, & acquisitions

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Technology Strategy (TS): defining questions illustrated with CISCO.
extracted_json.summary_gist: Notes frame Technology Strategy as a set of questions paired with a CISCO example. (i) Identifies the core technologies giving a company a technological advantage over competitors—for CISCO, advanced state-of-the-art Computer Network (CN) technologies like routers and switches for a wide variety of customers. (ii) Asks about the company's approach to product and technology development—CISCO is a world leader in CN technologies, driven by advanced R&D, new product development, and acquisitions.
